rss.title: Global forest loss slows but El Niño fires could threaten progress
rss.snippet: The loss of tropical rainforests eased last year, according to new analysis, but scientists warn they are still disappearing rapidly.

rss.title: £20m mystery gift buys London Zoo new hospital where you can watch vets work
rss.snippet: Visitors will be able to watch live veterinary procedures inside a state-of-the-art new animal hospital.

rss.title: UK's biggest ever environmental pollution claim reaches High Court
rss.snippet: One of the UK's largest chicken producers and a water company accused of polluting three rivers including the River Wye

arxiv_papers.title: Multiscale Decomposition Reveals Predictable Interannual Variability and Climate Trends in Antarctic Sea Ice Loss
arxiv_papers.snippet: Antarctic sea ice has undergone unprecedented changes in recent years, raising questions about how this key geophysical system is responding to climate change. arxiv_papers.title: Evaluating local climate in global storm-resolving models with the Köppen-Geiger classification
arxiv_papers.snippet: Global storm-resolving models aspire to become digital twins of the Earth, delivering information at the local scale at which humans experience climate. We evaluated how well two such models, ICON and IFS-FESOM, reproduce the climate as classified by the Köppen-Geiger system, using 30-year (2020-204

podcast: [A cheat sheet for accelerating clean energy | Kimiko Hirata] After the Fukushima disaster shut down Japan's nuclear reactors, the coal industry rushed in to fill the energy gap. As climate advocate Kimiko Hirata watched dozens of new coal plant proposals quietly surface across the country — each one locking in decades of future emissions — she resolved to make them impossible to ignore.
